annotations.label: Parallel postulate (original formulation)

anchor.snippet: If a straight line intersects two other straight lines forming two interior angles on the same side

annotations.note: Grep for 'parallel postulate' in Mathlib returns no hits; Mathlib's Euclidean geometry is analytic (inner product / affine spaces), not Euclid's synthetic axioms.

annotations.label: Parallel postulate (biconditional formulation)

anchor.snippet: the two interior angles on the same side add to less than two right angles if and only if the two lines

annotations.note: No synthetic axiomatic treatment of Euclid's postulates in Mathlib; no biconditional form appears.

annotations.label: Converse of the parallel postulate

anchor.snippet: If a straight line intersects two other straight lines that intersect on some side of first line

annotations.note: No converse-of-parallel-postulate statement found in Mathlib (no synthetic axiomatic geometry layer).

annotations.label: Euclidean geometry

anchor.snippet: Euclidean geometry is a geometry that satisfies all of Euclid's axioms

annotations.note: Mathlib has the `EuclideanGeometry` namespace built on inner product / affine spaces, which is a model of Euclidean geometry but not the axiomatic 'satisfies all of Euclid's axioms' definition.

annotations.label: Non-Euclidean geometry

anchor.snippet: Non-Euclidean geometries are geometries that do not satisfy the second form of the postulate

annotations.note: Searches for 'non-Euclidean', 'hyperbolic geometry', 'elliptic geometry' in Mathlib return no axiomatic-geometry hits (only hyperbolic functions/upper half-plane analysis).

annotations.label: Hyperbolic geometry

anchor.snippet: A hyperbolic geometry is a geometry that does not satisfy the original postulate

annotations.note: Mathlib mentions of 'hyperbolic' refer to hyperbolic functions and the upper half-plane metric, not the axiomatic notion of hyperbolic geometry.

annotations.label: Elliptic geometry

anchor.snippet: An elliptic geometry is geometry that does not satisfy the converse of the postulate

annotations.note: No definition of elliptic geometry as an axiomatic system appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spherical geometry: two lines meet in two points

anchor.snippet: in spherical geometry , two lines meet in exactly two points

annotations.note: Mathlib has no spherical-geometry framework with 'lines' as great circles; no such proposition is stated.

annotations.label: Absolute geometry

anchor.snippet: is known as an absolute geometry

annotations.note: Grep for 'absolute geometry' yields only unrelated hits (order, model theory); no synthetic absolute geometry.

annotations.label: Playfair's axiom

anchor.snippet: In a plane, given a line and a point not on it, at most one line parallel to the given line can be drawn through the point

annotations.note: Grep for 'Playfair' in Mathlib returns no results.

annotations.label: Equivalence of Playfair's axiom with parallel postulate

anchor.snippet: in the presence of the remaining axioms which give Euclidean geometry, one can be used to prove the other

annotations.note: Neither Playfair's axiom nor the parallel postulate is stated in Mathlib, so their equivalence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Statements equivalent to the parallel postulate

anchor.snippet: Many other statements equivalent to the parallel postulate have been suggested

annotations.note: No catalogue of statements equivalent to the parallel postulate exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euclid Book I, Proposition 27

anchor.snippet: If a straight line falling on two straight lines make the alternate angles equal to one another, the straight lines will be parallel to one another

annotations.note: No declaration in Mathlib states the alternate-interior-angles criterion for parallel lines in Euclid's synthetic form (only analytic angle/triangle lemmas exist).

annotations.label: Decomposition into Lotschnittaxiom and Aristotle's axiom

anchor.snippet: The parallel postulate is equivalent to the conjunction of the Lotschnittaxiom and of Aristotle's axiom

annotations.note: Grep for 'Lotschnitt' and 'Aristotle' in Mathlib geometry yields no results.

anchor.snippet: The former states that the perpendiculars to the sides of a right angle intersect
the latter states that there is no upper bound for the lengths of the distances from the leg of an angle to the other leg

annotations.label: Incidence form of Lotschnittaxiom

anchor.snippet: Given three parallel lines, there is a line that intersects all three of them

annotations.note: No corresponding incidence-geometry proposition is stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Incidence form of Aristotle's axiom

anchor.snippet: there exists a line g which intersects a and m , but not n

annotations.note: No such incidence-form statement is formalized in Mathlib.
